paper_title: Characterization of Wheat Straw-Degrading Anaerobic Alkali-Tolerant Mixed Cultures from Soda Lake Sediments

methods.description: Collection of sediment samples from two Hungarian soda lakes for further analysis.

steps.description: Sediment samples were collected from two Hungarian soda lakes, Lake Velencei and Lake Szarvas, in October 2011.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from specified locations

steps.description: Three sediment samples were taken from 1.5 m depth in Lake Velencei and 0.5 m depth in Lake Szarvas.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples at specified depths

steps.description: Sediment samples were taken with 100 ml glass bottles, filled with sediment and water, closed with airtight lids, and put into anoxic atmosphere generation bags.
steps.searchable_summary: store sediment samples in anoxic conditions

methods.description: Terminal Restriction Fragment Length Polymorphism (T-RFLP) analysis for characterizing microbial communities in sediment samples.

steps.description: Purified PCR products were digested overnight at 37°C with restriction endonucleases HaeIII or MspI.
steps.searchable_summary: digest PCR products with restriction enzymes

steps.description: Samples were ethanol precipitated, and dried DNA pellets were re-suspended in HiDi formamide.
steps.searchable_summary: precipitate and resuspend DNA in formamide

steps.description: Fluorescently labeled T-RFs were separated on an ABI PRISM 3130xl Genetic Analyser and analyzed using GENEMAPPER software.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze T-RFs using genetic analyzer and software

steps.description: Peak areas were normalized and noise filtering was applied, followed by multivariate cluster analysis using Bray-Curtis dissimilarity index.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize and analyze T-RFLP data with cluster analysis

methods.description: Cloning and sequencing of PCR products to identify microbial community composition.

steps.description: Cloning of purified non-labelled PCR products was carried out with QIAGEN PCR Cloning Kit.
steps.searchable_summary: clone PCR products using QIAGEN kit

steps.description: Vector-specific M13 primers were used to re-amplify the inserted 16S rRNA amplicons.
steps.searchable_summary: re-amplify 16S rRNA amplicons with M13 primers

steps.description: PCR products were screened by T-RFLP to select clones with inserts matching predominant peaks.
Partial sequencing of SAP-ExoI enzyme-treated PCR products was performed using Big Dye Terminator Ready Reaction Cycle Sequencing Kit.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence PCR products using Big Dye kit

steps.description: Almost full sequencing of 16S rRNA genes was achieved by assembling sequence information using SEQUENCHER software.
BLASTN and BLASTX algorithms were used to search for similar sequences in public databases.
steps.searchable_summary: search for similar sequences using BLAST

steps.description: Phylogenetic analyses including tree generation were conducted using MEGA4 software.
